surfaced_from: PWA request 2026-05-25 05:19 UTC — CISSP-like CTO security audit and prioritization
capability_needed: Rotate live service credentials and remove secret values from operational logs/history
details: The audit confirmed that CTO depends on live Hetzner, GitHub, OpenAI/OpenRouter, Hermes API, and PWA tokens in /opt/cto/.env. The current clone/install flow constructs candidate environment files by echoing values through shell scripts, which increases risk of leakage into command logs, process traces, session transcripts, or install artifacts. No secret values should be preserved in backlog or reports; treat this as a rotation and secret-handling redesign item.
search_trail: Ran openclaw status and openclaw security audit --deep on cto-v1; result: 0 critical, 1 warn, 1 info; gateway bound to 127.0.0.1 with token auth.
Ran ss -ltnup on cto-v1; found public SSH plus public HTTP/HTTPS and loopback-only OpenClaw/Hermes/A2A services.
Queried Hetzner API for servers, primary IPs, and firewalls; found no cloud firewalls and multiple public CTO servers/candidates.
Inspected /etc/caddy/Caddyfile without secrets; cto.husband.llc reverse-proxies to 127.0.0.1:8088 with security headers and access logging.
Scanned git history/current tree for secret-shaped patterns and key material; confirmed historical VAPID private key material and multiple documented credential-handling risks.
Checked file permissions for /opt/cto, ~/.openclaw, ~/.hermes, ~/.codex; sensitive home config dirs are mostly 0600/0700, while project files are group-writable by cto group.
Checked unattended-upgrades; service is enabled and active.
Attempted privileged sshd/fail2ban verification; elevated execution is unavailable in this PWA runtime, so privileged SSH posture remains unverified.
proposed_resolution: Rotate all live tokens after the public-repo audit completes, then replace shell-echo secret propagation with secure file transfer or a secrets manager pattern and redact existing operational logs where feasible. During the coordinated rotation window, evaluate CTO-DECISION-019 OpenAI workload identity federation as the preferred path for replacing long-lived OpenAI API keys if the current provider stack supports it; do not change runtime credentials before that window.
blast_radius: Rotating tokens can interrupt provisioning, embeddings, PWA auth, Hermes A2A, and GitHub/Hcloud automation until every dependent service is updated.
rollback_plan: Keep old credentials active only during a staged cutover window, update services one at a time, verify each path, then revoke old credentials.
resolution_notes:
2026-05-26: Multiple safe credential-handling substeps completed without rotating live credentials: operational redaction gates, git-history marker scanning, clone env streaming over SSH with restrictive permissions, Hetzner token argv hardening, installer config permission hardening, and a reusable install secret-handling guard added to safe security gates at 2026-05-26T23:25Z. Full staged live credential rotation and broader history/log cleanup remain open.

2026-05-27T00:25Z OpenClaw pump: advanced the safe non-destructive credential-hygiene track. Expanded operational log/chat redaction coverage for adjacent URL credential query parameters (access_token/auth_token/api_key/key), sensitive HTTP headers (X-API-Key/X-Auth-Token/X-Hermes-Token/HCloud-Token/GH-Token), and generic session/sid cookies. Added regression tests and verified the safe security gate suite; current log/chat redaction check scanned logs plus chat.db with no unredacted markers. Full closure still requires coordinated live credential rotation/revocation and any John-approved history/log cleanup.

2026-05-27T00:25Z: Expanded safe operational redaction coverage without rotating live credentials: URL credential parameter names beyond legacy token, sensitive HTTP header names, and generic session/sid cookie names are now redacted; regression coverage added. Safe security gates passed. Full closure still requires coordinated live credential rotation/revocation and approved broader cleanup.

2026-05-27T00:57Z: Added a non-destructive credential rotation preflight script that reports required credential names, optional/retired names to reconcile, env-file mode/owner, dependent service status, and coordinated rotation order without printing values or mutating runtime state. Verified syntax, live no-value output, and redaction check. Full closure still requires the coordinated live rotation/revocation window.

2026-05-27T01:10Z: Re-ran non-destructive credential-hygiene verification after the rotation preflight landed. Rotation preflight still reports required credential names present, .env mode 600, and dependent services active without printing values; safe security gates passed (secret artifact guard, operational redaction check, install guard, redaction tests, PWA auth/routing tests, PWA voice UI test). Standalone history-marker scan still reports known historical .vapid/private.pem markers until the coordinated public history scrub is approved. Full closure still requires live credential rotation/revocation and approved broader cleanup.

Full closure still requires coordinated live credential rotation/revocation and approved broader history/log cleanup.
